title: Render post markdown to sanitized HTML (pulldown-cmark + ammonia)
description: Implement the text → content_html pipeline. Step 1: parse with pulldown-cmark (CommonMark). Step 2: sanitize output with ammonia allowlist (a, br, p, strong, em, code, pre, ul, ol, li, blockquote — no script, no style, no onclick). Step 3: auto-link bare URLs. The render result is stored in posts.content_html. Agent workflow: Spawn a research subagent to collect a test corpus of Mastodon post formats (markdown, plain text, HTML with code blocks) and their expected rendered output from mastodon.social. Spawn a parallel subagent to build the ammonia allowlist configuration. Test discipline: - Test corpus from mastodon.social: render, compare to mastodon.social content field exactly - Test XSS attempt '' → ammonia strips to empty - Test '' → stripped - Test valid HTML passthrough 'bold' → preserved - Test nested markdown '*__both__*' → correct nesting - Test code block (backticks) → preserved - Each test uses mastodon.social rendered output as the oracle, NOT our own render function run twice Defensive programming: - ammonia: use Builder::new() with explicit allowlist — don't start from permissive defaults - Maximum rendered HTML length: if rendered HTML exceeds 100KB, truncate with '...' indicator - Track whether input was plaintext or markdown so we can preserve original in content field
acceptance_criteria: XSS attempts stripped; valid markdown rendered correctly per mastodon.social oracle; ammonia allowlist covers required tags; oversized content truncated
